claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 5 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 10 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To start, I need to understand a bit about your situation.

**Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, I'll need to know:
1. **How many children** do you want to check eligibility for?
2. **The age(s)** of your child(ren)?

This is important because Child Benefit eligibility depends on whether you have a "qualifying child," which is determined by their age and, in some cases, their education or training status.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have two children. I have Alex who is 5 years old, and Blake who is 10. They both live at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have two children:
- **Alex** (5 years old)
- **Blake** (10 years old)

Since both Alex and Blake are under 16, they potentially qualify as "qualifying children" for Child Benefit purposes. Let me check the next requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Now, the next question:

**Are your children working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

Given that Alex is 5 and Blake is 10, they're likely not working, but I need to confirm this to proceed with the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're not working at all. They're just kids - Alex is in primary school and Blake is too. Neither of them has any kind of job.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's perfect, thank you for confirming! Since neither Alex nor Blake are working, they meet this requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Are either of your children receiving any benefits in their own right** (such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit)?

Again, given their ages (5 and 10), this is very unlikely, but I need to confirm to continue the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them receives any benefits. They're just children - they don't get Universal Credit or ESA or anything like that in their own names.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent, thank you for confirming. Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Are either Alex or Blake currently in any form of apprenticeship?**

This would include things like:
- Apprenticeships in England (any level)
- Modern Apprenticeships in Scotland
- Apprenticeships in Wales (except Foundation level)

Given their ages (5 and 10), they're almost certainly not in apprenticeships, but I need to confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, of course not. They're only 5 and 10 - they're far too young for apprenticeships. They're both just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely, that makes complete sense! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about **your** residency status. This is an important requirement for claiming Child Benefit.

**Do you meet UK residency requirements?** You would qualify if ANY of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months)
4. You have pre-settled status and are actively seeking work (you can claim for up to 91 days)
5. You have pre-settled status with sufficient resources to support yourself and family
6. You have pre-settled status as a student with sufficient resources
7. You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

**Which of these applies to your situation?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester. I've always lived here, so yes, I meet the UK residency requirements.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! You meet the UK residency requirement as you normally live in the UK. Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to confirm your responsibility for Alex and Blake.

**Are you responsible for both children?** You mentioned earlier that they both live at home with you, so this sounds like it's already satisfied. Responsibility means:

1. **Living with the child** (which you've already mentioned), OR
2. Contributing to their upkeep by paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent) towards their care, OR
3. Paying maintenance under a court order or agreement

Since you said Alex and Blake both live at home with you, you meet this requirement. Can you confirm that's still the case?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. Both Alex and Blake live with me at home in Manchester. I look after them - they live with me full-time.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! You clearly have responsibility for both children as they live with you full-time. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check an important question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake?**

For example:
- Another parent or guardian
- A former partner
- Another family member

This is important because only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child at a time.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. It's just me looking after them and I haven't claimed it yet - that's why I'm checking if I'm eligible now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Since no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake, we can continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next, I need to check the living situation for Alex and Blake:

**Are either of your children currently:**
- In hospital?
- In care (looked after by the local authority)?
- Being fostered?
- Or are they living normally at home with you?

You mentioned they live at home with you, so I believe the answer is that they're living normally at home, but I need to confirm there are no special circumstances.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're just living normally at home with me. Neither of them is in hospital, in care, or being fostered. They're just regular kids living at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Since they're living normally at home, let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Final question:

**Is the local council paying towards either Alex or Blake's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, is the council contributing financially to where they live or their care costs? This would be different from you receiving other benefits - I'm specifically asking if the council is paying for the children's accommodation or maintenance.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance. I'm the one providing for them - they just live with me at home and I take care of all their needs.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! Based on all the information you've provided, you meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Great News - You're Eligible for Child Benefit!

**You meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

### Summary:
- ✅ **Alex (5 years old)**: Eligible
- ✅ **Blake (10 years old)**: Eligible

Both children qualify because they:
- Are under 16 years old
- Are not in paid work
- Are not receiving benefits in their own right
- Live with you at home

And you qualify because you:
- Normally live in the UK (Manchester)
- Have responsibility for both children (they live with you)
- Are not in a situation where someone else is claiming for them
- The children are living normally at home with you
- The local council is not paying towards their care

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and both children
3. **Provide birth certificates** (or adoption certificates if applicable) for Alex and Blake

### Important to Know:

- **Payments are made every 4 weeks** (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)
- **High Income Child Benefit Charge**: If you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay back some or all of the Child Benefit through your tax return. However, you can still claim it!

### How to Apply:
Visit: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

Is there anything else you'd like to know about claiming Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex (age 5) is eligible because: (1) Alex is under 16 years old, meeting the age requirement for a qualifying child; (2) Alex is not working (not in paid employment); (3) Alex is not receiving any benefits in their own right; (4) Alex is not in any form of apprenticeship; (5) Alex lives with the claimant who has responsibility for their care; (6) The claimant meets UK residency requirements (normally lives in Manchester); (7) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex is living normally at home (not in hospital, care, or fostered); (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are satisfied.

Blake (age 10) is eligible because: (1) Blake is under 16 years old, meeting the age requirement for a qualifying child; (2) Blake is not working (not in paid employment); (3) Blake is not receiving any benefits in their own right; (4) Blake is not in any form of apprenticeship; (5) Blake lives with the claimant who has responsibility for their care; (6) The claimant meets UK residency requirements (normally lives in Manchester); (7) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for Blake; (8) Blake is living normally at home (not in hospital, care, or fostered); (9) The local council is not paying towards Blake's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are satisfied.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for both children, Alex (5) and Blake (10). Both children meet all the qualifying child requirements as they are under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in apprenticeships. The claimant meets all claimant requirements including UK residency (living in Manchester), having responsibility for both children (they live together), no other person claiming for these children, children living normally at home, and the local council not paying towards their care. All eligibility criteria have been satisfied for both children.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
